Title: Leveraging AI for real-time inventory management in perishable goods supply chains
Source title: Modernizing the Food Industry: AI-Powered Infrastructure, Security, and Supply Chain Innovation
Affiliations: IBM, United States
Authors with affiliations: Rao V.V., IBM, United States
Abstract: Managing inventory in perishable goods supply chains presents unique challenges due to fluctuating demand, short shelf life, and the risk of spoilage. Traditional inventory management systems often struggle to maintain optimal stock levels while minimizing waste and ensuring product freshness. This chapter explores how Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming real- time inventory management in perishable goods supply chains. AI- driven forecasting models leverage machine learning and predictive analytics to enhance demand forecasting, automate replenishment, and optimize storage conditions. Computer vision and IoT- enabled sensors further contribute by monitoring product quality and providing real- time data for decisionmaking. Additionally, AI- powered automation and robotics streamline warehouse operations, reducing human errors and increasing efficiency. © 2026 by IGI Global Scientific Publishing. All rights reserved.

Title: Applications of Artificial Intelligence (AI) Across Diverse Functional Areas in Supply Chain Management (SCM)
Source title: Eco-Logistics and Sustainable Supply Chain Innovations
Affiliations: Kristu Jayanti College, India; Kristu Jayanti College of Law, India; Sri Sairam Engineering College, India

Abstract: This study examines the wide-ranging uses of artificial intelligence (AI) in many functional areas within supply chain management (SCM), providing insight into its significant impact on transformation. AI transforms operations in the supply chain, improving efficiency and resilience by implementing demand forecasting and logistics optimization. The present aims to study the application of artificial intelligence in the various functional areas of supply chain management. As per the convenience sampling method, the data are collected through structured questionnaire from 128 individuals who are the part of operational areas of supply chain management in Bangalore city. The collected data are analysed with descriptive statistics, ANOVA and regression. Finally, this study concludes that there is a positive effect of inventory management and control material and purchase management, demand estimation, order picking process, customer relationship management, transport network modelling on operational performance of supply chain management. © 2025 by IGI Global Scientific Publishing. Abstract: Purpose – The use of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) can potentially revolutionize logistics and supply chain management (SCM) practices. This research provides an analysis of how GenAI, huge language models (LLMs), are redefining SCM's operational and strategic aspects. Design/methodology/approach – A mixed-methods approach, incorporating case studies, industry surveys, and performance data analysis, is employed to investigate the inclusion of LLMs in logistics and SCM operations across various industries, including retailing, manufac-turing, and healthcare. Findings – The preliminary findings suggest that incorporating GenAI and LLMs into SCM systems enhances the speed of decision-making by 35%, accuracy of demand forecasting by 25%, and lowers logistics costs by 18%. Major innovations are dynamic route optimization, real-time inventory management, and customized customer service through AI-powered interfaces. Originality/value – The research makes an original contribution towards the digital evolution of SCM and logistics by presenting a sys-tematic approach to using LLMs. It highlights strategic recommendations for managers who wish to future-proof supply chains. © Dr. Chanduri Kiranmayi et al.

Title: Decision Support System for Inventory Management in Organizational Systems Based on Artificial Intelligence
Source title: Proceedings - ICSES 2026: 5th International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems
Affiliations: College of Computer Science and Math, Tikrit University, Tikrit, Iraq
Authors with affiliations: Arnawtee M.M.M., College of Computer Science and Math, Tikrit University, Tikrit, Iraq; Ali M., College of Computer Science and Math, Tikrit University, Tikrit, Iraq; Fadhelzaiter M., College of Computer Science and Math, Tikrit University, Tikrit, Iraq
Abstract: In the modern digital economy, efficient inventory management has become a critical factor in maintaining the competitiveness and operational efficiency of companies, especially those operating in dynamic and technology-driven markets. The increasing complexity of global supply chains, the volatility of consumer demand, and the growing volume of data generated by sales channels have necessitated the adoption of advanced information technologies for inventory forecasting and optimization. Among these technologies, artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) methods have shown great promise in improving the accuracy and efficiency of inventory prediction models [1]. On AI-based decision support systems for inventory management is underlined by a set of converging elements that have positioned inventory optimization as a strategic priority and not an operational problem. Firstly, inventory represents a significant capital expense for organizations in any industry, with the global inventory management software market expected to reach $3.2 billion by 2026, underscoring the significant economic relevance of the domain. Second, the pandemic of COVID-19 has disclosed significant vulnerabilities of global supply chains, ruthlessly illuminating the consequences of overstocking (wastage and obsolescence) and poor inventory levels (stockouts and forgone sales), thereby more forcefully emphasizing the need for more resilient and responsive inventory control practices [2]. Third, the resultant exponential rise in data availability-through enterprise resource planning systems, Internet of Things sensors, and digital supply chain platforms-has created unprecedented opportunities for sophisticated analytics previously not possible, but most organizations are struggling to leverage this data abundance to derive meaningful inventory insights. Fourth, despite the advances in machine learning and AI, research indicates that almost 79% of companies still significantly rely on spreadsheets or basic software for inventory choice, indicating a colossal gap between technological potential and actual adoption. Fifth, increasing pressure for sustainability across business activities means enhanced precise inventory management to reduce waste, reduce carbon emissions, and improve circular economic practices [3]. Sixth, inventory positioning has become more difficult with e-commerce and omnichannel retailing and decreased the desirable level of inventory imbalance, as the customer expects instant availability of the product through every channel. Finally, academic research has tended to focus on individual building blocks of inventory management-demand forecasting, optimization models, risk analysis, or pricing policy-without much regard for overall frameworks that deal with the interdependencies between these components [4].  © 2026 IEEE.

Title: A Conceptual Study on Amazon’s Use of AI to Enhance Transparency in Its Supply Chain, Ensuring Sustainable and Ethical Sourcing and Distribution
Source title: Lecture Notes in Electrical Engineering
Affiliations: Kristu Jayanti College, Bengaluru, India
Authors with affiliations: Glady Agnes L., Kristu Jayanti College, Bengaluru, India; Manoj Prabhakar M., Kristu Jayanti College, Bengaluru, India; Jemmima L., Kristu Jayanti College, Bengaluru, India; Raghavendra Babu J., Kristu Jayanti College, Bengaluru, India
Abstract: In today’s rapidly evolving global marketplace, efficient and supply chain management is imperative for the success of e-commerce giants like Amazon. This study highlights Amazon’s adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies to optimize its supply chain operations. Leveraging AI’s capacity for predictive analytics, automation, and data-driven decision-making, Amazon has revolutionized supply chain processes, from demand forecasting and inventory management to logistics and last-mile delivery. This paper provides an in-depth exploration of the AI-driven initiatives employed by Amazon, showcasing their profound impact on supply chain efficiency and customer satisfaction. Through AI, Amazon has achieved remarkable advancements in demand prediction accuracy, enabling precise inventory allocation, and reducing carrying costs. Furthermore, machine learning algorithms have transformed order fulfillment, enabling faster and more reliable deliveries while minimizing operational costs. The study also highlights Amazon’s utilization of AI-powered robotics and automation within its fulfillment centers, enhancing picking, and packing processes. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Singapore Pte Ltd. 2025.

Affiliations: Big Data, Enterprise and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory, University of the West of England, Bristol, United Kingdom; Department of Digital Technologies (Pearson) Global Banking School, Birmingham, United Kingdom; Greater Manchester Business School (GMBS), University of Bolton, Bristol, BL3 5AB, United Kingdom

Abstract: This chapter examines the use of artificial intelligence, data analytics and other digital technologies in the management of the supply chain decision-making. The study highlights the challenges faced by supply chain managers and how the application of AI and data analytics can help in making better and more informed decisions with respect to sustainability. Data analytics, AI techniques, such as machine learning, natural language processing and other digital technologies that include Internet of Things, Robotics and Cloud computing and their applications to different areas of supply chain management, such as demand forecasting, inventory management and logistics optimisation are discussed. Some of the challenges (initial cost of physical and cloud resources, change management, ethical and legal-related issues) that the supply chain managers need to put into consideration when adopting these technologies are also presented. The chapter concludes that continuous data collection and storage across all the stakeholders in the supply chain must be ensured to enable transparent and efficient use of AI algorithms to support quick and timely supply chain decision-making. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Singapore Pte Ltd 2024.

Title: Optimizing inventory management through AI-driven demand forecasting for improved supply chain responsiveness and accuracy
Source title: AIP Conference Proceedings
Affiliations: Department of Information Technology, University of the Cumberlands, Williamsburg, KY, United States
Authors with affiliations: Sajja G.S., Department of Information Technology, University of the Cumberlands, Williamsburg, KY, United States; Addula S.R., Department of Information Technology, University of the Cumberlands, Williamsburg, KY, United States; Meesala M.K., Department of Information Technology, University of the Cumberlands, Williamsburg, KY, United States; Ravipati P., Department of Information Technology, University of the Cumberlands, Williamsburg, KY, United States
Abstract: Efficient inventory management is crucial for sustaining the stability and profitability of supply chains, especially amid globalized markets and fast technological progress. Conventional inventory management techniques, while efficient in stable environments, fail in addressing the unpredictability of contemporary supply chains, characterized by demand fluctuation, seasonality, and frequent interruptions. The incorporation of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into demand forecasting offers a viable answer to these difficulties. AI-driven models, using techniques like as machine learning, deep learning, hybrid approaches, and reinforcement learning, significantly enhance conventional methods by delivering more precise, dynamic, and responsive demand projections. These models can evaluate intricate data patterns, adjust to real-time changes, and respond to market swings, leading to optimum inventory levels, diminished holding costs, and fewer stockouts. Machine learning models such as Decision Trees, Random Forests, and Gradient Boosting may evaluate extensive datasets, discerning patterns to forecast future demand based on economic indicators, sales trends, and other variables. Deep learning models, like Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, are especially proficient in time-series forecasting, since they effectively capture sequential dependencies essential for industries with cyclical demand. Hybrid models, integrating AI with statistical techniques such as ARIMA, improve prediction precision by capturing both linear trends and non-linear patterns within the data. Reinforcement learning models are essential for continually refining inventory techniques via real-time feedback, hence assisting in the maintenance of appropriate stock levels. The incorporation of AI-powered demand forecasting into Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems and inventory management software automates stock replenishment and facilitates the study of real-time data from many sources, enhancing supply chain responsiveness. Nonetheless, despite the benefits of AI-driven forecasting, other obstacles must be confronted, including guaranteeing data quality and accessibility, handling the substantial computing demands of intricate models, and enhancing the openness of AI models, especially in regulated sectors. Addressing these problems will be essential for the extensive use of AI-driven solutions in inventory management. This papers examines the capacity of AI-driven demand forecasting to enhance inventory management, augment supply chain responsiveness, and decrease costs. The document offers an extensive examination of several AI methodologies, including machine learning, deep learning, hybrid strategies, and reinforcement learning, while evaluating their influence on forecasting precision and supply chain efficacy. © 2025 Author(s).

Title: Artificial Intelligence-Enabled Supply Chain Management: Unlocking New Opportunities and Challenges
Source title: Artificial Intelligence and Applications
Affiliations: Department of Mechanical Engineering, Abacus Institute of Engineering and Management, India; Department of Mechanical Engineering, Durgapur Institute of Advanced Technology and Management, India; Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indira Gandhi Institute of Technology, India
Authors with affiliations: Goswami S.S., Department of Mechanical Engineering, Abacus Institute of Engineering and Management, India; Mondal S., Department of Mechanical Engineering, Abacus Institute of Engineering and Management, India; Sarkar S., Department of Mechanical Engineering, Durgapur Institute of Advanced Technology and Management, India; Gupta K.K., Department of Mechanical Engineering, Abacus Institute of Engineering and Management, India; Sahoo S.K., Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indira Gandhi Institute of Technology, India; Halder R., Department of Mechanical Engineering, Abacus Institute of Engineering and Management, India
Abstract: This paper delves deeper into the potential of Artificial Intelligence (AI)-enabled supply chain management (SCM) as a ground breaking technology capable of revolutionizing supply chain operations and ushering in a new era of possibilities. In today’s dynamic business landscape, where agility and efficiency are paramount, AI plays a pivotal role in redefining how supply chains operate. The journey commences with an in-depth exploration of AI’s fundamental concepts and its manifold applications within SCM, shedding light on its adaptability across various aspects of the supply chain, from demand forecasting to inventory optimization. Moreover, this paper illuminates the myriad benefits that AI brings to SCM practitioners. These advantages encompass heightened operational efficiency through real-time data analysis, cost reduction through predictive maintenance and optimized routing and a superior customer experience resulting from improved demand prediction and personalized service offerings. However, acknowledging the transformative power of AI in SCM, we must also acknowledge the hurdles in its implementation. This paper underscores the significant challenges that organizations may face while integrating AI into their SCM processes, ranging from data quality issues and concerns regarding privacy and security to the need for domain-specific human expertise. To address these hurdles effectively, the paper proposes a comprehensive framework. This framework encompasses a holistic strategy that aligns AI initiatives with organizational goals, governance, and ethics considerations to ensure responsible AI deployment, and a clear roadmap that guides the implementation journey from inception to full integration. In conclusion, this paper offers valuable insights into the opportunities and challenges that AI-powered SCM presents in the ever-evolving business landscape. By providing practical recommendations, it equips organizations with the knowledge and tools needed to successfully harness the potential of AI in their supply chain operations, ultimately paving the way for enhanced competitiveness and sustainability in the future. © The Author(s) 2024.

Authors: Bala P.K.
Author full names: Bala, Pradip Kumar (26639138400)
Title: Improving inventory performance with clustering based demand forecasts
Source title: Journal of Modelling in Management
Affiliations: Xavier Institute of Management, Bhubaneswar, India
Authors with affiliations: Bala P.K., Xavier Institute of Management, Bhubaneswar, India
Abstract: Purpose–The purpose of this paper is to develop a forecasting model for retailers based on customer segmentation, to improve performance of inventory. Design/methodology/approach–The research makes an attempt to capture the knowledge of segmenting the customers based on various attributes as an input to the demand forecasting in a retail store. The paper suggests a data mining model which has been used for forecasting of demand. The proposed model has been applied for forecasting demands of eight SKUs for grocery items in a supermarket. Based on the proposed forecasting model, the inventory performance has been studied with simulation. Findings–The proposed forecasting model with the inventory replenishment system results in the reduction of inventory level and increase in customer service level. Hence, the proposed model in the paper results in improved performance of inventory. Practical implicationsRetailers can make use of the proposed model for demand forecasting of various items to improve the inventory performance and profitability of operations. Originality/value–With the advent of data mining systems which have given rise to the use of business intelligence in various domains, the current paper addresses one of the most pressing issues in retail management, as demand forecasting with minimum error is the key to success in inventory and supply chain management. The proposed forecasting model with the inventory replenishment system results in the reduction of inventory level and increase in customer service level. The proposed model outperforms other widely used existing models. © 2012, © Emerald Group Publishing Limited.

Title: AI-driven demand forecasting for sustainable inventory model in fuzzy environment
Source title: Sustainable Futures
Affiliations: Department of Mathematics, Amity Institute of Applied Sciences, Amity University Uttar Pradesh, Noida, India; Department of Mathematics, School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology, Bennett University, Greater Noida, Uttar Pradesh, India; Department of Computer Science, Amity School of Engineering and Technology, Amity University Uttar Pradesh, Noida, India
Authors with affiliations: Chaudhary R., Department of Mathematics, Amity Institute of Applied Sciences, Amity University Uttar Pradesh, Noida, India; Mittal M., Department of Mathematics, School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology, Bennett University, Greater Noida, Uttar Pradesh, India; Arora H.D., Department of Mathematics, Amity Institute of Applied Sciences, Amity University Uttar Pradesh, Noida, India; Jindal K., Department of Computer Science, Amity School of Engineering and Technology, Amity University Uttar Pradesh, Noida, India; Chaudhary N.K., Department of Mathematics, Amity Institute of Applied Sciences, Amity University Uttar Pradesh, Noida, India
Abstract: Inventory management in the present world’s supply chains has to overcome challenges such as uncertain product demand, defective items, and market-related uncertainties. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and advanced models that use fuzzy logic offer solutions to these challenges. This research showcases a framework that uses Random Forest to predict a product’s demand based on the past sales data, capturing seasonal variability, growth and other factors such as holidays, discount offers, etc., to improve inventory planning. while using inventory models that use fuzzy logic to account for uncertainty in the amount of defective items. The proposed model contains various processes, namely item inspection, defective item disposal processes, and backordering, providing a reliable and strong framework for real-world applications. The model promotes sustainability as it also considers the carbon emissions generated during the supply chain. The model is tested using the specified numerical examples. And the effect of varying different parameters on different outputs of the model is examined in sensitivity analysis. Finally, managerial implications contain strategies that will optimize order quantities while reducing costs and maximizing profits for retailers. However, this article’s USP is how AI’s predictive capabilities, typically used in industries like finance, healthcare, and manufacturing, can revolutionize supply chain management. © 2026 The Authors. Authors: Arif J.; Jawab F.
Title: Transformative Impact of AI in Supply Chain Management
Source title: Transformative Impact of AI in Supply Chain Management
Affiliations: Sidi Mohamed Ben Abdellah University, Fez, Morocco
Authors with affiliations: Arif J., Sidi Mohamed Ben Abdellah University, Fez, Morocco; Jawab F., Sidi Mohamed Ben Abdellah University, Fez, Morocco
Abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) plays a transformative role in shaping the future of supply chains. AI-driven technologies, including machine learning (ML), predictive analytics, and autonomous systems, are revolutionizing traditional supply chain processes. They enable real-time decision-making, demand forecasting, risk management, and end-to-end visibility for Supply Chain 4.0. As a result, AI can enhance supply chain resilience, sustainability, and efficiency in the digital and increasingly globalized market. The strategic application of AI will not only reduce operational costs but also contribute to long-term organizational resilience and sustainability goals. Transformative Impact of AI in Supply Chain Management has broad implications for multiple sectors, providing deep insights into the transformative potential of AI in supply chain operations. It highlights the role of AI in achieving sustainability, optimizing routes and inventory management, and creating agile supply chains that can respond dynamically to market fluctuations and disruptions. Covering topics such as industrial prosperity, integrated business planning (IBP), and social sustainability, this book is an excellent resource for logistics specialists, supply chain managers, industrial engineers, operations executives, technology developers, professionals, researchers, scholars, academicians, and more. © 2026 by IGI Global Scientific Publishing. All rights reserved.

Authors: Tiwari N.; Prasad S.K.
Author full names: Tiwari, Nitin (60184420100); Prasad, Subrat Kumar (60183767500)
Title: Agentic AI-Driven Optimizing Demand Forecasting in Retail Systems with AI-Based Predictive Analytics
Source title: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Vision, AIMV 2025
Affiliations: Jamia Millia Islamia, Alumnus Department of Computer Engineering, New Delhi, India; IIT Roorkee, Alumnus Department of Mathematics, Uttarakhand, Roorkee, India
Authors with affiliations: Tiwari N., Jamia Millia Islamia, Alumnus Department of Computer Engineering, New Delhi, India; Prasad S.K., IIT Roorkee, Alumnus Department of Mathematics, Uttarakhand, Roorkee, India
Abstract: This study examines the utilization of optimization techniques in retail forecasting based on models developed from artificial intelligence technology. The prime motive here is to improve the efficiency and accuracy of prediction through AI implementation and breakthroughs in resource optimization and inventory control. This approach, through machine learning algorithms, effectively combines past data, determines people's shopping patterns, responds to changes in market conditions, and delivers forecasts of greater accuracy. The conclusions point to the superior performance of AI-based models over conventional methods such as ARIMA, ETS, and different methods, with a 20% savings in inventories and a 15% gain in the availability of products. Additionally, this study stresses the importance of continuous calibration of models and real-time data input to maintain forecasting accuracy. The broader implications point to the use of artificial intelligence-based forecasting as highly promising in bringing significant operational efficiencies, cost savings, and improvements in customer satisfaction. Henceforth, retailers might look to improve profitability and supply chain optimization through these advanced forecasting abilities. © 2025 IEEE.

Author full names: Abueed, Omar (57551035500); Yaeesh, Osamah (59369935200); AlAlaween, Wafa′ H. (57190953879); Abueed, Saleh (60264368500); AlAlawin, Abdallah H. (55818997000)
Title: A Systematic AI-Based Paradigm for Classifying Hybrid Electric Vehicle Spare Parts Using Their Price and Demand
Source title: Journal of Engineering (United Kingdom)
Affiliations: School of Systems Science and Industrial Engineering, Binghamton University, Binghamton, NY, United States; Department of Industrial Engineering, The University of Jordan, Amman, Jordan; Faculty of Engineering, Al-Balqa’ Applied University, Amman, Jordan; Department of Industrial Engineering, Faculty of Engineering, The Hashemite University, Zarqa, Jordan
Authors with affiliations: Abueed O., School of Systems Science and Industrial Engineering, Binghamton University, Binghamton, NY, United States; Yaeesh O., School of Systems Science and Industrial Engineering, Binghamton University, Binghamton, NY, United States; AlAlaween W.H., Department of Industrial Engineering, The University of Jordan, Amman, Jordan; Abueed S., Faculty of Engineering, Al-Balqa’ Applied University, Amman, Jordan; AlAlawin A.H., Department of Industrial Engineering, Faculty of Engineering, The Hashemite University, Zarqa, Jordan
Abstract: With the rapid growth of the hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) market, stakeholders must refine after-sales logistics, particularly spare parts (SPs) provisioning. Adequate forecasting of demand and pricing underpins these efforts. This paper presents a feedforward artificial neural network (FF-ANN) that, drawing on 15 predictor variables, assigns each SP to low, medium, or high categories for both demand and price. The resulting scheme enables judicious inventory management, optimized procurement, and reduced operational spending by synchronizing supply with realistic forecasts. Explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques were then incorporated to enhance model interpretability by identifying key influencing factors. The results indicated that factors such as failure rate, number of cars, car age, and average total maintenance cost have a high relative impact on demand prediction outcomes, whereas factors such as part type, online price, and the new/used parts have high impacts on the price prediction model. Authors: Weraikat D.; Al-Hourani S.
Author full names: Weraikat, Dua (57044794100); Al-Hourani, Shireen (34881467000)
Title: Systematic Review of Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Pharmaceutical Supply Chain (PSC) Resilience: Identifying Gaps and Future Research Directions
Source title: Communications in Computer and Information Science
Affiliations: Rochester Institute of Technology, Dubai, Dubai, United Arab Emirates; University Canada West, Vancouver, Canada
Authors with affiliations: Weraikat D., Rochester Institute of Technology, Dubai, Dubai, United Arab Emirates; Al-Hourani S., University Canada West, Vancouver, Canada
Abstract: The resilience of the pharmaceutical supply chain (PSC) is crucial to ensure the continuous availability of essential medicines and other products, particularly during logistics disruptions. This article presents a systematic review of the literature on machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI) techniques and their importance to PSC resilience. By analyzing existing literature from multiple databases, including Web of Science and IEEE Xplore, over the past five years, we identified key areas where ML and AI have been effectively utilized. These areas include demand forecasting, risk management, and inventory optimization. This review also highlights significant research gaps and proposes future directions for investigation. Our findings suggest that while ML and AI offer promising solutions for improving supply chain resilience, there is a need for more studies that integrate various ML and AI approaches into PSC. Our analysis reveals that there are no clear government regulations related to the usage of ML or AI in PSC, no robust real-world applications addressing the challenges of adopting these technologies, and no clear predictive models to assess their impact on PSC resilience. This ongoing work aims to provide a foundation for future research, ultimately fostering more adaptive and resilient PSCs. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2026.

Author full names: Suura, Sambasiva Rao (59920245700); Chava, Karthik (59682243300); Chakilam, Chaitran (60022019100); Malempati, Murali (59733881300); Nuka, Sai Teja (58765422800)
Title: Revolutionizing Retail Supply Chains with AI and Cloud Computing: A Big Data Approach to Demand Forecasting and Logistics Optimization
Source title: Lecture Notes in Networks and Systems
Affiliations: Natera Inc, Austin, TX, United States; Knipper, Princeton, NJ, United States; Sequel Medtech, Manchester, NH, United States; Mastercard International Inc, O’Fallon, MO, United States; Argon Medical, Plano, TX, United States
Authors with affiliations: Suura S.R., Natera Inc, Austin, TX, United States; Chava K., Knipper, Princeton, NJ, United States; Chakilam C., Sequel Medtech, Manchester, NH, United States; Malempati M., Mastercard International Inc, O’Fallon, MO, United States; Nuka S.T., Argon Medical, Plano, TX, United States
Abstract: In today’s dynamic retail landscape, supply chain management is a crucial component for ensuring efficiency, cost reduction, and customer satisfaction. The adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) and cloud computing has significantly transformed traditional supply chain operations by enabling real-time decision-making, predictive analytics, and logistics optimization. However, existing supply chain models often face challenges such as high operational costs, demand uncertainty, inefficient inventory management, and suboptimal logistics planning, leading to increased lead times, stock imbalances, and higher defect rates. To mitigate these inefficiencies, this research presents an AI-driven predictive and prescriptive analytics framework that integrates machine learning, reinforcement learning, and cloud-based optimization to enhance demand forecasting, inventory control, and logistics efficiency. Experimental findings demonstrate that XGBoost improves demand forecasting accuracy, reducing forecasting errors and optimizing stock levels. Furthermore, reinforcement learning enhances inventory replenishment and real-time route optimization, significantly lowering transportation costs. AI-powered supplier performance assessment and IoT-enabled shipment tracking improve supply chain visibility and minimize delays. The results validate that AI-driven methodologies enhance supply chain agility, minimize operational risks, and optimize cost efficiency, making them highly scalable and adaptable for real-world retail applications. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2026.

Title: A review on the adoption of AI-powered supply chain and logistics management for effective organizational performance in smes in India
Source title: Cross-Industry AI Applications
Affiliations: Jain University, India
Authors with affiliations: Ulle R.S., Jain University, India; Yogananthan S., Jain University, India; Kumar V.V., Jain University, India; Reddy T., Jain University, India
Abstract: The strategic integration of AI-powered supply chain and logistics management is fast becoming a linchpin for elevating organizational performance within small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). In a landscape where operational excellence, fiscal prudence, and customer-centricity are paramount, AI technology emerges as the catalyst for SMEs to reimagine their supply chain and logistics processes. By harnessing the formidable capabilities of artificial intelligence and internet of things (IoT)-enabled technologies, SMEs stand to reap substantial benefits across various dimensions of their operations, spanning precise demand forecasting, agile inventory management, efficient route optimization, and real-time monitoring, and also resulting in reducing cost and thus improving the returns on investments. This visionary incorporation of AI-powered supply chain management and logistics not only streamlines processes but also promises a remarkable transformation of organizational performance as a whole. © 2024 by IGI Global.

Authors: Bhavikatta N.B.; Goluguri N.V.R.
Author full names: Bhavikatta, Naga Bharadwaj (60131276500); Goluguri, Naga Venkata Rajareddy (60582277500)
Title: Multi dataset LSTM analysis for accurate and sustainable supply chain forecasting
Source title: Discover Sustainability
Affiliations: Cary, NC, United States; University of Saskatchewan, Saskatoon, SK, Canada
Authors with affiliations: Bhavikatta N.B., Cary, NC, United States; Goluguri N.V.R., University of Saskatchewan, Saskatoon, SK, Canada
Abstract: Accurate demand forecasting is a critical enabler of sustainable Supply Chain Management, as it directly influences production planning, inventory control, resource utilization, and environmental impact. In alignment with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, particularly SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), SDG 12 (Responsible Consumption and Production), and SDG 13 (Climate Action), this study proposes a layered AI-based forecasting framework aimed at enhancing supply chain efficiency while supporting sustainable development objectives. The proposed framework integrates three progressively advanced models: a standard Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network, an LSTM with an Attention Mechanism, and a Quantum-inspired Attention LSTM, designed to capture long-term temporal dependencies and complex feature interactions inherent in real-world supply chain data. The framework is experimentally validated using three real-world supply chain datasets representing e-commerce, grocery retail, and customer analytics domains. Experimental results demonstrate that the Quantum-inspired Attention LSTM consistently outperforms classical LSTM and attention-based LSTM models, achieving forecasting accuracies of 92.85%, 92.56%, and 90.42%, respectively. Improved forecasting accuracy directly contributes to sustainability by reducing overproduction, minimizing excess inventory, lowering avoidable transportation and storage operations, and mitigating carbon emissions across supply chain networks. An ablation study further confirms that the incorporation of attention and quantum-inspired representations significantly enhances model robustness and predictive reliability. The findings illustrate that advanced AI-driven forecasting, when explicitly aligned with SDG-oriented objectives, can serve as a practical and scalable mechanism for enabling sustainable supply chain operations, supporting data-driven decision-making, and advancing responsible production and consumption practices. © The Author(s) 2026.

Authors: Shrikumar M.; Kurien G.P.; Saha S.K.; Sharma K.
Author full names: Shrikumar, M. (59415138400); Kurien, Georgy P. (56613231000); Saha, Saibal Kumar (57215305982); Sharma, Kapila (57226311454)
Title: Development of an AI Based Framework for Reverse Supply Chain of Pharmaceutical Products
Source title: Lecture Notes in Networks and Systems
Affiliations: Christ University, Bangalore, India; St. Xavier’s University Kolkata, New Town Action Area 3, West Bengal, Kolkata, 700160, India; Sikkim Manipal Institute of Technology, Majitar, India
Authors with affiliations: Shrikumar M., Christ University, Bangalore, India; Kurien G.P., Christ University, Bangalore, India; Saha S.K., St. Xavier’s University Kolkata, New Town Action Area 3, West Bengal, Kolkata, 700160, India; Sharma K., Sikkim Manipal Institute of Technology, Majitar, India
Abstract: The pharmaceutical reverse supply chain is an integral part of pharmaceutical industry. Due to the complex nature of the process and strict government regulations, it is important to use different AI technologies to increase the efficiency of the reverse supply chain. This research aims to design an AI driven framework for reverse supply chain of pharmaceutical products which would increase efficiency, speed, automate processes and enhance trust among the stakeholders. The framework consists of five modules namely Collection and Sorting Centre, Return Management, Real-time Inventory Management, Disposal Centre, and Data Analytics. In each module different AI technologies have been embedded to increase the efficiency of the system. The proposed framework offers a holistic approach that not only aligns with stringent pharmaceutical standards but also contributes to a more robust, transparent, and environmentally sustainable reverse supply chain. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2026.

Authors: Ezmigna I.; Alghizzawi M.; Habes M.; Salloum S.A.
Author full names: Ezmigna, Ibrahim (58524879600); Alghizzawi, Mahmoud (57998843800); Habes, Mohammad (56453227500); Salloum, Said A. (57195670894)
Title: Application of Data Mining and AI for Supply Chain Inventory Forecasting: A Qualitative Approach
Source title: Studies in Computational Intelligence
Affiliations: Azman Hashim International Business School, University Technology Malaysia, Johor, Pagoh, Malaysia; Digital Marketing, Marketing Department, Applied Science Private University, Amman, Jordan; Radio & TV Department, Faculty of Mass Communication, Yarmouk University, Irbid, Jordan; School of Computing, Skyline University College, Sharjah, United Arab Emirates
Authors with affiliations: Ezmigna I., Azman Hashim International Business School, University Technology Malaysia, Johor, Pagoh, Malaysia; Alghizzawi M., Digital Marketing, Marketing Department, Applied Science Private University, Amman, Jordan; Habes M., Radio & TV Department, Faculty of Mass Communication, Yarmouk University, Irbid, Jordan; Salloum S.A., School of Computing, Skyline University College, Sharjah, United Arab Emirates
Abstract: This study provides a literature review on data mining, artificial intelligence (AI) and supply chain inventory forecasting which is the subject of this paper. Using a mixed-method approach, this paper investigates the strategic significance of these technologies in improving accuracy of forecasts and agility in operations. The study uses a hybrid of thematic and quantitative analysis of interviews and data patterns from SCM professionals to identify the advantages and disadvantages of AI and data mining in-order-fulfillment and inventory management. Results indicate that AI and data analytics tools are effective at handling complex demand patterns; however, implementation barriers—mostly data quality, IT system integration, and workforce capabilities—exist to fully benefit from it. This research study will provide valuable recommendations to companies who have intentions of future use of AI and data mining for the purposes of inventory forecasting along with avenues for future research in SCM technology. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2025.

Author full names: Sharma, Himanshu (58945726900); Sahu, Neeta (58890377600); Singhal, Ritesh Kumar (57219490251); Garg, Ankit (57660818100); Singhal, Rahul (57201758642); Tripathi, Swati (59423708300)
Title: Data-Driven Forecasting and Inventory Optimization using Machine Learning Models and Methods
Source title: 2024 1st International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies, ACET 2024
Affiliations: Ajay Kumar Garg Institute of Management, Ghaziabad, India; Institute of Management Studies, Ghaziabad, India
Authors with affiliations: Sharma H., Ajay Kumar Garg Institute of Management, Ghaziabad, India; Sahu N., Ajay Kumar Garg Institute of Management, Ghaziabad, India; Singhal R.K., Ajay Kumar Garg Institute of Management, Ghaziabad, India; Garg A., Ajay Kumar Garg Institute of Management, Ghaziabad, India; Singhal R., Ajay Kumar Garg Institute of Management, Ghaziabad, India; Tripathi S., Institute of Management Studies, Ghaziabad, India
Abstract: The growing availability of big data and machine learning has led to a focus on data-driven solutions in Operations and Supply Chain Management (OSCM). This research aims to develop predictive and prescriptive analytics models to address challenges in supply chain forecasting and inventory optimization. The proposed Demand Forecasting Model (DFM) combines big data analytics and machine learning to provide accurate forecasts for operational and tactical decisions. A novel Demand Forecasting Decision Model (DFDM) is developed to provide long-term forecasts where big data models are not applicable. The research also explores the applicability of human judgment in the era of artificial intelligence. A novel supply chain forecasting framework is proposed, combining temporal and cross-sectional hierarchical forecasting to generate coherent forecasts at all levels of a supply chain. A data-driven inventory optimization model is developed, solving the multi-item inventory optimization problem with limited capacity/budget using machine learning and optimization. The proposed models and methodologies have numerous applications across various industries, including fashion, food, energy, electronics, and media. The proposed models and methodologies have numerous applications across supply chains of various industries.  © 2024 IEEE.

Title: AI-Based Mathematical Modelling for Sustainable Business and Inventory Management
Source title: 2024 International Conference on Smart Technologies for Sustainable Development Goals, ICSTSDG 2024
Affiliations: Graphic Era, Deemed to Be University, Uttarakhand, Dehradun, India; Symbiosis Institute of Computer Studies and Research, Symbiosis International (Deemed University), Pune, India; Uttaranchal Institute of Management, Uttaranchal University, Dehradun, India; Uttaranchal School of Computing Sciences, Uttaranchal University, Dehradun, India; Dept of Commerce, Graphic Era Deemed to be University, Dehradun, India

Abstract: The paper focuses on the elaboration and application of AI-based mathematical models for sustainable inventory management, which have the full potential to improve both operational efficiency and environmental performance. The integration of machine learning algorithms, optimization techniques, and real-time data in the proposed models will help improve accuracy in demand forecasting, diminish waste in inventory, and reduce carbon emissions within supply chains. Indeed, a 20-25% improvement in demand forecasting, a reduction of inventory costs by 15%, and waste generation as high as 30%. Furthermore, AI-driven sustainability metrics, such as the efficiency of transport and energy consumption, have been implemented that show quantifiable measures of carbon footprint reduction for the activities involved in managing inventories. The findings hence indicate that AI provides a transformative way of associating business goals and objectives with those that are sustainable. Conclusions are drawn on broader implications for business sustainability and point toward further research that may aim to optimize the role of AI in sustainability and business operations. © 2024 IEEE.

Title: Optimization of Green Finance Using AI and Blockchain in Inventory Management
Source title: 2024 International Conference on Smart Technologies for Sustainable Development Goals, ICSTSDG 2024
Affiliations: Graphic Era, Deemed to Be University, Uttarakhand, Dehradun, India; Uttaranchal Institute of Management, Uttaranchal University, Dehradun, India; SOM Graphic Era Hill University, Dehradun, India; Dept of Commerce, Graphic Era Deemed to be University, Dehradun, India

Abstract: Study examines positions assumed by Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, and Green Finance to see how each separately and collectively might play a role in the inventory management systems to contribute to sustainability and financial efficiency. AI, in its data-driven predictive power, optimizes the processes of inventory through improved demand forecasting, reduced waste, and resource usage. Blockchain ensures much-needed transparency, traceability, and accountability throughout value chains, enabling the verifiability of more precise sustainability metrics via smart contracts, which can automate Green Finance agreements. Integration of these technologies serves to align inventory management toward sustainability goals of reduced carbon footprint and energy efficiency. The paper reviews the literature, examines key models and frameworks, and discusses the potentials of AI and Blockchain to influence sustainable practices and financial outcomes in inventory management. Future research opportunities are outlined to address current gaps and enhance the adoption in real-world applications of the subject technologies. © 2024 IEEE.

Title: Optimization Of Supply Chains and Marketing in The Agricultural Sector Using Innovative Technologies
Source title: Pakistan Journal of Agricultural Research
Affiliations: Department of Electronic Marketing and Social Media, Zarqa University, Zarqa, Jordan; Department of Business Administration, Irbid National University, Irbid, Jordan; Department of Business management, University of Jerash, Jerash, Jordan; Donetsk National Technical University, Ukraine; College of Communication and Media, Al Ain University, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates; Department of Applied Marketing, King Saud University, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia

Abstract: This study investigates the potential of advanced technologies to enhance supply chains in family owned agricultural businesses. Special emphasis is placed on improving transparency, efficiency, and sustainability in agri-food production through smart digital technologies such as blockchain, IoT, big data, artificial intelligence, and automation. The study aims to identify the challenges and opportunities of digitalization in agriculture and explore how these technologies can facilitate more efficient interactions among all stakeholders in the agricultural supply chain. To address these objectives, we propose an integrated model for optimizing supply chains using digital technologies. This includes a set of mathematical models and algorithms designed to minimize costs, increase efficiency, and ensure traceability across all stages of goods movement from producer to end consumer. The methodology incorporates mathematical modelling techniques and assesses the impact of digital technologies on various stages of supplier performance, including production and sales. The results demonstrate that advanced technologies can significantly reduce cycle times, enhance precision in agricultural production, lower logistics costs, and improve process transparency. Specifically, blockchain and IoT enhance product traceability, while big data analytics enables better demand forecasting and inventory management. Additionally, digitization strengthens collaboration among farms, slaughterhouses, and consumers. The originality of this work lies in its interdisciplinary approach, integrating insights from agronomy, logistics, and digital technologies. The paper offers a fresh perspective on the role of innovative technologies in supply chain optimization and examines the potential risks agricultural companies may encounter when adopting such solutions. The study provides actionable recommendations for agricultural producers, logistics firms, and government agencies on implementing digital supply chain solutions based on mathematical models. The findings can inform strategies for agricultural digitalization and contribute to improving the efficiency and sustainability of the sector amid global changes. © Copyright: 2025 by the authors. Title: Does disruptive technology and AI (Artificial Intelligence) influence logistics management?
Source title: Multidisciplinary Science Journal
Affiliations: Department of Commerce, School of Social Sciences and Languages, Vellore Institute of Technology, Vellore, India; Solutions Architect, Williams Lea, London, United Kingdom
Authors with affiliations: Indradevi R., Department of Commerce, School of Social Sciences and Languages, Vellore Institute of Technology, Vellore, India; Natarajan S., Department of Commerce, School of Social Sciences and Languages, Vellore Institute of Technology, Vellore, India; Sathyamoorty V.K.P., Solutions Architect, Williams Lea, London, United Kingdom
Abstract: Disruptive technologies like AI, automation, and IoT are transforming logistics and distribution management, driving efficiency, cost savings, and improved customer experiences. This empirical investigation investigates the role of these technologies in optimizing operations, enhancing decision-making, and building resilient supply chains. The study analyzes the impact of disruptive technologies on various aspects of logistics, including inventory management, route optimization, predictive maintenance, and last-mile delivery. By leveraging real-time data and advanced analytics, AI algorithms enable accurate demand forecasting, automated replenishment systems, and personalized delivery options, leading to increased efficiency and customer satisfaction. The integration of IoT sensors facilitates real-time tracking of shipments and environmental conditions, enhancing visibility and enabling proactive issue resolution. However, ethical considerations surrounding AI, workforce transformation, and cybersecurity pose challenges that require careful navigation. Study survey was conducted among 216 people from logistics and distribution sector to know "role of disruptive technology and artificial intelligence in effective logistics and distribution management." The study concludes that there is a significant impact of disruptive technology and artificial intelligence on logistics and distribution management. © 2024 Malque Publishing. All rights reserved.
Author Keywords: customer-centric logistics; distribution management; green logistics; internet of things; logistics; supply chain dynamics

Authors: Habib F.; Bourlakis M.
Author full names: Habib, Farooq (57197972264); Bourlakis, Michael (6506844873)
Title: E-procurement in retailing
Source title: Elgar Encyclopedia of Retailing
Affiliations: Cranfield School of Management, Cranfield, United Kingdom
Authors with affiliations: Habib F., Cranfield School of Management, Cranfield, United Kingdom; Bourlakis M., Cranfield School of Management, Cranfield, United Kingdom
Abstract: Faced with ever increasing competition and escalating costs, retailers are seeking potential opportunities to overcome these hurdles by incorporating e-procurement. The key elements in this entry are the following: (a) Artificial Intelligence (AI) is playing a pivotal role in unlocking significant volumes of data generated due to retail activity to improve demand forecasting accuracy; (b) Machine Learning (ML) driven models are enabling retailers create value through inventory optimisation; (c) Block chain technology enhances the level of transparency and traceability across the entire retail ecosystems; (d) Robotic Process Automation (RPA) is employed to reduce errors by replacing many of the manual procurement procedures; (e) A combination of Internet of Things (IoT), block chains, and radio frequency identification (RFID) tags is employed by retailers to track the real-time movement of goods and services in real time across their supply network; (f) Retailers are managing their administrative cost associated with supplier-relationship management by automating many of the low value adding activities in their procurement process; (g) The use of artificial intelligence and advance heuristics empower retailers to conduct spend cube analysis and identify hidden savings opportunities in their spend management workflow. © Editors and Contributors Severally 2025.
Author Keywords: Demand forecasting; E-procurement; Inventory management; Spend cube analysis; Supplier relationship management; Tracking and tracing

Title: Forecasting Demand and Optimizing Product Ordering in the Supply Chain Using Artificial Intelligence
Source title: International Journal of Knowledge Processing Studies
Affiliations: Department of Business Management, Rasht Branch, Islamic Azad University, Rasht, Iran
Authors with affiliations: Pakbin M., Department of Business Management, Rasht Branch, Islamic Azad University, Rasht, Iran; Ghofrani Y.R., Department of Business Management, Rasht Branch, Islamic Azad University, Rasht, Iran; Shahroodi K., Department of Business Management, Rasht Branch, Islamic Azad University, Rasht, Iran
Abstract: The purpose of this research is to predict demand and optimize product ordering within the supply chain using artificial intelligence. Employing a purposeful sampling method, 12 managers from Kale were selected as the sample group. This study utilized two Delphi techniques and a neural network. Through semi-structured interviews conducted in a fuzzy Delphi panel, relevant components were identified. Predictions were made using the Multi-Layer Perceptron Neural Network toolbox in MATLAB software. The results from the fuzzy Delphi analysis indicated that the primary factors influencing the forecast included warehouse inventory, sales from the previous week, sales from the previous month, cargo in transit, fluctuations in customer numbers compared to the past, competitors' market status, government regulations, and the company's development plans. After finalizing the Delphi process, the key factors identified were warehouse inventory, sales from the previous week, sales from the previous month, changes in customer numbers compared to the past, competitors' market status, and government regulations. The neural network predictions demonstrated that, due to the fluctuating demand trends, the predicted values closely aligned with the actual values. According to the graphs and the average squared error values, the neural network's predictions were deemed acceptable, even with the rapid fluctuations in actual demand. In the case of Kale Dairy Company, utilizing artificial intelligence for demand forecasting and optimizing product ordering can yield significant benefits. Firstly, more accurate demand forecasting through artificial intelligence algorithms can lead to a reduction in waste and excess inventory within the supply chain. This enables the company to better identify and meet customer needs, ultimately resulting in increased customer satisfaction. © authors.
Author Keywords: Artificial Intelligence; Demand Forecasting; Order Optimization; Supply Chain Management

Authors: Dindarian A.
Author full names: Dindarian, Azadeh (44261142900)
Title: Harnessing AI and Big Data to Build a Resilient Supply Chain: An Overview
Source title: International Series in Operations Research and Management Science
Affiliations: SRH Berlin University of Applied Sciences, Berlin, Germany
Authors with affiliations: Dindarian A., SRH Berlin University of Applied Sciences, Berlin, Germany
Abstract: This chapter explores the transformative role of digital technologies, including Artificial Intelligence (AI), Big Data and Internet of Things (IoT) in shaping supply chain management. As global supply chains grow more complex and vulnerable to disruptions such as geopolitical tensions and global crises, the demand for resilient, adaptive, and efficient systems continues to rise. AI-powered tools, predictive analytics, and real-time data insights enable organizations to shift from reactive problem-solving to proactive risk management, enhancing visibility, agility, and decision-making across the supply chain. Drawing from both academic literature and industry case studies, the chapter offers a well-rounded view of current AI applications in building supply chain resilience. It examines real-world examples from companies like Amazon and Tesco, showcasing how technologies such as demand forecasting, inventory optimization, route planning, and predictive maintenance are being used for competitive advantage. While the benefits are clear, digital transformation also brings challenges. Legacy systems, data quality issues, workforce upskilling, and ethical concerns must be addressed with thoughtful strategies, investment, and robust data governance to ensure sustainable, responsible adoption of these technologies. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2026.
Author Keywords: Artificial intelligence; Big data; Data analytics; Digital transformation; Supply chain resilience

Title: Reshaping Supply Chain Management Using Artificial Intelligence

Authors with affiliations: Rattan P., School of Computer Application, Lovely Professional University, Phagwara, Punjab, India
Abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing supply chain management (SCM) by enhancing efficiency, accuracy, and adaptability. AI-powered predictive analytics optimize demand forecasting, reducing inventory costs and minimizing stockouts. Machine learning algorithms streamline logistics by identifying optimal routes, reducing transportation costs, and improving delivery timelines. Automated systems and robotics enhance warehouse operations, boosting productivity and minimizing human error. Additionally, AI-driven supplier risk management enables proactive decision-making by analyzing vast datasets for potential disruptions. Blockchain-integrated AI strengthens supply chain transparency and security by preventing fraud and ensuring data integrity. As AI continues to evolve, its integration into SCM promises increased resilience, cost reduction, and enhanced customer satisfaction. Organizations that adopt AI-driven SCM strategies gain a competitive edge in a rapidly evolving global market. The future of supply chains lies in AI-driven automation, predictive intelligence, and agile decision-making. © 2026 by Apple Academic Press, Inc.

Title: AI-Driven Optimization of Supply Chain and Logistics in Mechanical Engineering

Abstract: Mechanical engineering businesses rely heavily on effective supply chain and logistics management to increase their productivity, efficiency, and competitiveness. Recent years have seen the rise of artificial intelligence (AI) approaches as potent instruments for improving logistics and supply chain operations. This abstract gives a thorough introduction to Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), an AI method inspired by nature, and how it may be used to improve mechanical engineering's supply chain and logistics. The foraging strategies of ants served as inspiration for the development of Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), a metaheuristic algorithm. It has garnered a lot of interest as a useful tool for supply chain and logistics optimization because of its capacity to tackle difficult optimization challenges. Inventory management, transportation routing, production scheduling, and demand forecasting are just some of the mechanical engineering problems that may be tackled with the help of ACO. Inventory optimization is a key use case for ACO in the context of mechanical engineering supply chain management. By modeling how ants locate food sources, ACO is able to ascertain optimum stock levels. To cut down on carrying costs and stockouts, it helps find the sweet spot between overstocking and understocking of raw materials and finished goods. Likewise, transportation route optimization is greatly aided by ACO. Transporting both inputs and outputs quickly and cheaply is crucial for factories. Taking into account variables like traffic, fuel prices, and delivery windows, ACO can determine the most efficient routes for trucks. This not only improves customer satisfaction through on-time deliveries but also decreases transportation expenses. Mechanical engineers may also use ACO to enhance production scheduling. Algorithms for Achieving Maximum Efficiency (ACOs) may plan the flow of production such that downtime, wasted materials, and lost revenue are kept to a minimum. Mechanical engineering firms may boost output and shorten manufacturing times by optimizing their production plans. Despite the inherent uncertainty in demand forecasting, ACO can improve prediction accuracy. Algorithms for adaptive costing and optimization (ACO) can aid mechanical engineering companies in making better judgments on production volumes and inventory levels by assessing past demand data and continuously revising forecasts based on real-time information. Overproduction and underproduction are avoided, resulting in cost savings and better service to customers. ACO may also be utilized to improve the process of finding and working with vendors. It may take into account several criteria, including supplier dependability, cost, and turnaround time, to select the most suitable vendors for mechanical engineering businesses. In addition to lowering material acquisition costs, this also guarantees a steady supply of high-quality raw materials. In conclusion, ACO-driven AI optimization of mechanical engineering's supply chain and logistics has several advantages, including lower costs, more efficiency, and happier clients. Companies in the mechanical engineering sector can gain an edge by implementing ACO algorithms into their inventory management, transportation routing, production scheduling, demand forecasting, and supplier selection processes. To remain competitive and resilient in the ever-changing area of mechanical engineering, the use of AI techniques like ACO will become increasingly vital as technology progresses.  © 2023 IEEE.

Title: APPLICATIONS OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE TOOLS IN LOGISTICS AND SUPPLY CHAIN MANAGEMENT
Source title: Logforum
Affiliations: Department of Logistics, Warsaw, University of Life Sciences – SGGW, Poland; School of Business, University of Economics and Human Sciences, Warsaw, Poland; Department of Communications, Faculty of Operation and Economics of Transport and Communications, University of Žilina, Slovakia
Authors with affiliations: Michalski K., Department of Logistics, Warsaw, University of Life Sciences – SGGW, Poland; Karcz J., School of Business, University of Economics and Human Sciences, Warsaw, Poland; Madleňáková L., Department of Communications, Faculty of Operation and Economics of Transport and Communications, University of Žilina, Slovakia
Abstract: Background: Artificial intelligence (AI) is poised to profoundly reshape logistics and supply chain management in the 21st century—much as the steam engine, electricity, and computers transformed industries in earlier eras. AI is recognised as a key driver in developing next-generation supply chains, enhancing organisational agility, and improving delivery efficiency. It supports a wide range of operational and managerial functions, including preventive, reactive, and corrective actions within logistics systems. Most importantly, AI reduces the risk of human error, thereby mitigating economic and business losses and lowering operational costs. This article aims to identify the most critical areas of AI application in logistics and supply chain management, explore its broader potential uses, and discuss solutions to implementation challenges, with particular emphasis on the human factor. As a holistic review, the study contributes to the literature by mapping current AI applications, highlighting emerging trends, and underscoring human-centric challenges, thereby providing a foundation for further research. Methods: A systematic literature review was conducted following the SALSA (Search, Appraisal, Synthesis, and Analysis) approach to address the research gap in understanding the practical applications of AI tools in logistics and supply chain management. Results: The analysis revealed five key directions for implementing AI technologies in logistics and supply chains. Human factors—and the related phenomenon of “dehumanisation”—emerged as critical challenges for managers overseeing transformations that extend beyond IT integration towards the systemic adoption of AI in operations and management. Conclusions: The potential applications of AI in logistics are diverse, encompassing demand forecasting, inventory management, delivery route optimisation, resources planning, and process monitoring, among others. Each of these applications influences the role of humans within organisations and operational processes, requiring strategic, tactical, and operational adaptation. The study identifies its limitations and proposes directions for future research on AI applications in logistics and supply chain management. Human-machine interaction should remain a central focus of future investigations into AI-driven transformations. © Wyższa Szkoła Logistyki, Poznań, Polska.

Title: Convergence of GenAI and RPA: A Digital Heaven in Supply Chain Optimization Process
Source title: Journal of Computer Science
Affiliations: Cardinal Health, Dublin, Ohio, United States; Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, State University of New York Polytechnic Institute, United States; Independent Researcher, United States; Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Marwadi University, India; Department of Computer Science and Engineering, University of Alaska Anchorage, United States

Abstract: One of the most groundbreaking advances in the field of intelligent automation is the merging of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) and Robotic Process Automation (RPA). This combination unlocks previously unachievable capabilities in automation. RPA (software bots) improves speed and operational efficiency by automating rule-based tasks, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), and repetitive workflows. At the same time, it minimizes manual labor and human errors. While, GenAI's advanced proficiencies in Natural Language Processing (NLP) with context, content creation, and smart decision-making make it well suited or tasks that require flexibility and adaptability. Leveraging the fusion of the two can empower greater improvements in the supply chain world. Overall, in the supply chain management workflow, this convergence drives automation to new heights and delivers the precision, and adaptability across the end-to-end process. This integration enables real-time forecasting of multiple level items to each item/eaches, case quantities, pallet load, inventory management optimization, demand forecasting, customer service and communication, logistics and transportation management, anomaly detection, damage detection, etc. in the supply chain process. This paper explores in brief the future potential of supply chain automation using the fusion of GenAI and RPA, along with practical applications, challenges, and future directions. © 2025 Saurabh A. Title: AI-Driven Supply Chains: Revolutionizing Efficiency and Risk Management
Source title: Proceedings - ICSES 2026: 5th International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems
Affiliations: Blue Yonder Argyle, TX, United States; Microsoft, Austin, TX, United States; Honeywell Inc, OH, United States; Carolina University, Winston, NC, United States; Dallas, TX, United States
Authors with affiliations: Kotha N.K., Blue Yonder Argyle, TX, United States; Premalatha M.A.K., Microsoft, Austin, TX, United States; Kanniyappan S.K., Honeywell Inc, OH, United States; Owolabi O., Carolina University, Winston, NC, United States; Handaragal R., Dallas, TX, United States
Abstract: Today's supply chains are more complex, globalized and subject to more disruptions, but they need new approaches to efficiencies and changing risk levels: A great number of traditional supply chain strategies don't efficiently and dynamically respond to changing demand, inventory balances, and external disruptions to create greater efficiency at lower costs and service levels. This research seeks to establish an artificial intelligence-based framework that combines predictive modelling, reinforcement learning and network-based risk detection in order to optimise the supply chain operations. The framework takes advantage of historical and operating data sets, and uses LSTM for demand forecasting, RL agents for prescriptive decision making, and graph neural networks for disruption detection. Comparative analysis shows that there is a significant improvement in some of the following aspects: lead time: 32 hours, inventory cost: $ 8.6 M, order fulfillment accuracy: 96.8%, disruption probability: 3.2%, expected loss: $ 1.2 M, resilience index: 0.89, superior model performance metrics (MAE=4.5, F1 -score =0.91, cumulative RL reward =1340). The proposed AI framework is a robust data-driven framework for supply chain efficiency and resilience enhancement that can offer actionable insights to operation managers and policy-makers in such a dynamic industrial context.  © 2026 IEEE.

Abstract: This study examines the use of artificial intelligence (AI) and Internet of Things (IoT) in operations management, namely inventory management. Operations management is crucial to maximizing productivity and net operational profit for enterprises. The global shift to industrial revolution 4.0, AI and IoT’s crucial roles is the main focus. AI and IoT are useful for demand forecasting, real-time tracking, and risk management in inventory management in supply chain management. Inventory risk management, demand forecasting, and real-time tracking are the main issues of this study. The real-time tracking emphasizes responsiveness, timeliness, adaptability, and speed. Supply Chain Risk Management (SCRM) classifies hazards by their impact on supply chain network levels and subsets. The analysis reveals that, due to the combination of AI and real-time tracking technology, supply chain management has advanced, especially in inventory management. IoT integration in modern supply chain management enables proactive issue resolution, early barrier discovery, and fast response to real-time data, improving logistics operating efficiency. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Singapore Pte Ltd. 2025.

Title: Rfqv-Based Demand Forecasting for Retail Stores
Source title: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computing for Sustainability and Intelligent Future, COMP-SIF 2026
Affiliations: Acharya Institute of Technology, Dept of Aiml, Bengaluru, India
Authors with affiliations: Deglus J., Acharya Institute of Technology, Dept of Aiml, Bengaluru, India; Pavan Kumar V., Acharya Institute of Technology, Dept of Aiml, Bengaluru, India; Tejashree S.P., Acharya Institute of Technology, Dept of Aiml, Bengaluru, India; Ranjita R., Acharya Institute of Technology, Dept of Aiml, Bengaluru, India; Sahoo P.R., Acharya Institute of Technology, Dept of Aiml, Bengaluru, India
Abstract: Demand forecasting is an essential activity in the retail supply chain management discipline, where precise predictions ensure the maintenance of optimal levels of inventory and the minimization of operational expenses. Nevertheless, traditional approaches to demand forecasting fail to effectively address the intricate nature of customer purchasing behavior. This research presents a novel RFQV-based framework for demand forecasting, where the model is based on the incorporation of Recency, Frequency, Quantity, and Variation features from historical retail transaction data. The research aims to enhance the accuracy of demand predictions through the integration of customer behavior indicators and demand pattern classification. The proposed framework classifies the pattern of the product's demand as smooth, erratic, lumpy, and intermittent demand, ensuring the adoption of efficient models such as Prophet, XGBoost, and LightGBM for the prediction task. The proposed model is evaluated based on the accuracy of the predictions using metrics such as MAE, MAPE, sMAPE, and R2. The results show that the proposed RFQV-based framework improves the accuracy of the predictions and minimizes prediction errors compared to traditional approaches to the task. The proposed model presents an efficient mechanism for retailers to make informed decisions in the optimization of their operational activities.  © 2026 IEEE.

Abstract: Artificial Intelligence and its subdivision, Machine Learning pose the question of the effective incorporation of intelligent technology into supply chain and marketing functions. Controlling these constraints and achieving efficiency, accuracy, and scalability in handling the rise in data volumes require a deeper understanding of AI and ML’s bi- end applications in these essential areas and displays how both can be implemented explicitly in decreasing processes, assigning resources efficiently and obtaining comprehensive analytical results. The learning process is supported by the real examples of AI and ML application in demand forecasting, intelligent inventory management, logistics systems, personalized marketing, and the analysis of customers’ behavior. Thus, by outlining how these technologies can be integrated strategically within a business, this chapter seeks to help organisations to innovate and sustain competitive advantage in the ever- evolving sphere of supply chain and marketing. © 2025 by IGI Global Scientific Publishing. Authors: Hu Y.; Ghadimi P.
Author full names: Hu, Yang (58026728500); Ghadimi, Pezhman (54419924400)
Title: A Review of Artificial Intelligence Application on Enhancing Resilience of Closed-loop Supply Chain
Source title: Proceedings of the 29th International Conference on Engineering, Technology, and Innovation: Shaping the Future, ICE 2023
Affiliations: University College Dublin, Laboratory for Advanced Manufacturing Simulation and Robotics, School of Mechanical & Materials Engineering, Dublin, Ireland
Authors with affiliations: Hu Y., University College Dublin, Laboratory for Advanced Manufacturing Simulation and Robotics, School of Mechanical & Materials Engineering, Dublin, Ireland; Ghadimi P., University College Dublin, Laboratory for Advanced Manufacturing Simulation and Robotics, School of Mechanical & Materials Engineering, Dublin, Ireland
Abstract: The purpose of this review is to investigate the landscape of scientific literature in analysing the Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology capabilities for enhancing supply chain resilience both from the forward and reverse side in times of increased risk and uncertainty. This article adopts a systematic literature analysis to extract and analyse the related papers that implemented AI in typical SC functions that can help develop supply chain resilience. It is found that AI technology can be considered as enabling technology for mitigating disruption risk in the closed-loop supply chain due to higher efficiency and accuracy in supplier selection, facility allocation, inventory planning, capacity planning, and demand forecasting, leading to resilience and robustness of a closed-loop supply chain. This review also suggested that a more comprehensive, in-depth, and wide range study from both forward and reverse, especially the reverse side from the reactive, dynamic aspects should be conducted in the future to better reduce supply chain risk more efficiently, effectively, and completely.  © 2023 IEEE.

Title: Construction and Emergency Response Application of Knowledge Graph
Source title: Proceedings - 2025 IEEE Smart World Congress, SWC 2025, 2025 IEEE Ubiquitous Intelligence and Computing, Autonomous and Trusted Computing, Digital Twin, Metaverse, Scalable Computing and Communications
Affiliations: Lanzhou University of Finance and Economics, School of Information Engineering and Artificial Intelligence, Lanzhou, China
Authors with affiliations: Chen L., Lanzhou University of Finance and Economics, School of Information Engineering and Artificial Intelligence, Lanzhou, China; Zhang K., Lanzhou University of Finance and Economics, School of Information Engineering and Artificial Intelligence, Lanzhou, China
Abstract: Emergency response preparedness is the core element that enhances the effectiveness of emergency management, and it possesses comprehensiveness, systematicity, authority, and practicality. This paper aims to improve its data support and preventive disposal capability through artificial intelligence, especially using Neo4j and Python, multi-feature fusion, Tokenizer word segmentation, direct mapping method, manually defined rules, long and short term memory network(LSTM), and content analysis to construct a knowledge graph. After knowledge extraction, the raw data was structured and 19 kinds of entities with 15 kinds of relationships were extracted. Through knowledge fusion and knowledge storage, the knowledge graph of emergency plans is successfully constructed. This system not only helps managers to optimize inventory management and demand forecasting, but also improves the efficiency of the supply chain and reveals the correlation between materials. © 2025 IEEE.
